_TEMPLATE_NEW_MONTH._instrucciones: Copy this block to add a new month. Replace the key with the month (e.g. 'Aug-26'). Write 3-5 highlights and 3-5 lowlights. Body supports numbers, %, $X.Xm, bps.

highlights.title: Net Income
highlights.body: Net Income $X.Xm vs Budget $Y.Ym.

highlights.title: Financial Margin
highlights.body: Financial Margin $X.Xm, +X.X% vs Budget.

lowlights.title: +61 Days Past Due
lowlights.body: +61 bucket at X.X% of portfolio, above target.

lowlights.title: Renewal Rate
lowlights.body: Renewal Rate (Board) at XX.X% vs target.

highlights.title: Lead Generation & Conversion
highlights.body: 11.1k new client applications (+46.0% MoM), out of which 9.9k were new client applications and 1.1k were re-engagements of applications, despite a lower budget of MXN $248k (–2.8% MoM; –23.8% vs. Q4'25 avg.). Performance was primarily driven by a cost-free Uber blast, which generated 3.1k. Importantly, beyond volume growth, efforts remain focused on improving lead quality.

highlights.title: Referral Program
highlights.body: Since the official launch in August, the referral program has yielded 819 referrals, out of which 174 have resulted in originations, still representing the most efficient conversion channel with a 21.1% conversion rate (vs. the avg. conversion rate of 4.2%).

highlights.title: Origination
highlights.body: Signed 175 new vehicle leases (-18.6% MoM; +20.7% vs. Budget) and 28 used vehicle leases (+75.0% MoM; -6.7% vs. Target), out of which 100 were EVs (57.1% of total new leases), leading to a net portfolio of MXN$ 971.3m (+1.3% MoM; +0.6% vs. Budget). Originated 121 new leases in Uber (69.1% of total) and 54 new leases in DiDi (30.9% of total). During Jan 136 Uber EVs completed their first trip (including originations from previous months) 33.7% of Q1 target and 5.1% of the ‘26 target.

highlights.title: Active Fleet
highlights.body: Reached 3,155 vehicles (+3.4% MoM; +6.2% vs. Budget) out of which 1,487 are active EVs (46.1% of active fleet, +5.6% MoM), with 1,198 corresponding to Uber (80.6%; 341 light and 857 Premium) and 289 to DiDi (19.4%, 161 light and 128 Premium).

highlights.title: Branding
highlights.body: Branded 100% of new EV originations during the period and integrated branding data into the CRM, enabling a targeted outreach campaign to drivers with unbranded vehicles, resulting in 48 additional brandings. Total branded fleet for Uber reached 235 vehicles (123.7% of the 190 target). We are now preparing for the February ramp-up, targeting 380 Uber and 39 for DiDi.

highlights.title: VEMO Impulso App
highlights.body: Reached a total of 3,358 registered users, with 2,650 Monthly Active Users (MAU) and 2,100 Weekly Active Users (WAU), (+17.8% MAU MoM and +57.9% WAU MoM).

highlights.title: Strategic Developments (Uber & DiDi)
highlights.body: (i) Uber: We expect to land CAPEX grants for 2.5k Light and 500 Premium EVs (quantum and split in line with our BYD order) and are pushing for discretionally to go further on Premium EVs. This would equate to at least MXN$ 65m in total, vs. 10.8m in budget and (ii) DiDi: negotiated an increase in the multiplier from 1.3x to 1.8x, applicable across all trip categories. This enhancement is intended to improve driver earnings and, in practice, allow drivers to exceed the MXN$200 RPH guarantee.

lowlights.title: Portfolio
lowlights.body: Past due portfolio (+90 days) represented 3.7% (+14 bps MoM) of the total portfolio.

lowlights.title: DAE-LTO Program
lowlights.body: Transitioned 34 DAE drivers (32.0% below target).

lowlights.title: Repossessions
lowlights.body: We completed 102 effective repossessions during the period (+35 units MoM), reflecting recoveries across multiple cohorts and operational stages. Closed the month with 60 vehicles pending repossession, including carryover from prior periods. To improve execution and move toward the 7-day SLA proposed last month, we focused on: (i) enhanced coordination between Fleet and Collections teams; (ii) earlier initiation of repossession requests by Collections; (iii) prioritization of vehicles with active GPS reporting and faster recovery potential; and (iv) sending a single, comprehensive weekly case list to the repossession provider to streamline coordination and accelerate execution.

lowlights.title: Used Vehicle Inventory
lowlights.body: Increased to 312 units (9.1% of fleet, +73 units MoM), comprised of 173 EVs and 139 ICE-HEV. This was mainly driven by: (i) 102 effective repossessions; (ii) 28 used vehicle originations; and (iii) the reclassification of 1 vehicle as a substitute vehicle.

lowlights.title: Collections
lowlights.body: Closed at 95.4% (-5 bps MoM), with Uber collections at 99.9% (+130bps MoM, +290 bps above target) and DiDi collections at 91.5% (-250 bps MoM, -550 bps below target). DiDi´s collection underperformance was mainly driven by: (i) pressure from the EV Premium bucket; (ii) DiDi´s retention tool did not collect as expected starting on Jan 12th; and (iii) Apr–Jul'25 suboptimal vintages still normalizing.

lowlights.title: DiDi Collection Tool
lowlights.body: Out of the 518 active DiDi leases, we have registered 390 clients under the collection tool (75.3% of total). Of these, 283 clients (58.6% of total) have not accepted retention via DiDi, 115 clients (23.8% of total) have retention fully active, and 5 clients (1.0% of total). On Jan 12th, the activation of 38 drivers took place.

lowlights.title: Collection Initiatives Update
lowlights.body: Enhance operativity of the following initiatives: (i) a skip-a-payment option that allows clients to defer one payment per year; and (ii) disaster relief program that postpones payment up to 4 weeks due to unforeseen situations such as severe illness, surgery or natural disasters.

highlights.title: Referral Program
highlights.body: Since the official launch in August, the referral program has yielded 886 referrals, out of which 196 have resulted in originations, still representing the most efficient conversion channel with a 22.0% conversion rate (vs. our avg. conversion rate of 4.2%).

highlights.title: DAE-LTO Program
highlights.body: Transitioned 63 DAE drivers (24.0% above target), mainly driven by improved communication of the benefits of our LTO EV product.

highlights.title: Active Fleet
highlights.body: Reached 3,215 vehicles (+3.3% MoM; +4.8% vs. Budget) out of which 1,577 are active EVs (49.1% of active fleet, +7.6% MoM), with 1,275 corresponding to Uber (80.8%; 307 light and 905 Premium) and 302 to DiDi (19.2%, 170 light and 132 Premium).

highlights.title: Branding
highlights.body: Branded 95 vehicles during the month (79 Uber, 16 DiDi), bringing the total branded fleet for Uber to 314 (82.6% of the 380 target) and 52 for DiDi vehicles.

highlights.title: Product Developments
highlights.body: (i) Optimized GPS and Maintenance costs for legacy clients achieving a reduction of ~MXN $250/week per EV (ca. 4.7% saving); and (ii) new product design featuring a lower cost legacy product and new 36-month lease with 20% residual value, both expected to launch during March.

highlights.title: Uber Capex Grants
highlights.body: We expect to land CAPEX grants for 2.5k Light and 500 Premium EVs (quantum and split in line with our BYD order) and are pushing to go further on Premium EVs. This would equate to at least MXN $65m in total, vs. MXN $12.6m in budget. Of note, Uber introduced a two-prong clawback: (i) minimum 2,500 rides per year (already embedded in our prior LTO contracts); and (ii) a deployment shortfall clawback (the “Partner Engagement Mechanism”) if we fail to deliver the 3k vehicles, driven by their concern about EVs migrating to DiDi.

highlights.title: Uber Black Category
highlights.body: Uber has approved the BYD Yuan Pro for the Uber Black category across the 500 committed units over the full contract term and will allow all legacy premium EVs (excluding BYD D1) to remain in Uber Black for the duration of their respective leases. For BYD D1 units, Uber has verbally committed to a revenue support mechanism to preserve driver earnings despite their exclusion from the Black category, expected to be implemented post-World Cup.

highlights.title: Rappi Prospect
highlights.body: Initiated commercial conversations with founder and other senior stakeholders to structure an LTO product targeting Rappi's ~25,000 “Diamond” couriers (top 25% by engagement and earnings) in Mexico. Rappi has indicated willingness to support the program, and we are exploring six potential levers: (i) lead generation; (ii) CAPEX grants; (iii) revenue guarantee; (iv) priority dispatch to ensure trip volume covers the guarantee with healthy unit economics; (v) API integration for data sharing; and (vi) direct collections from their platform. The opportunity also provides a natural channel to place BYD Dolphin Minis which count towards our agreement with BYD, as well as reducing origination concentration risk on Uber/DiDi. Key open items: guarantee structure tied to connected hours / orders made, acceptance rates and quality metrics, anti-gaming mechanisms, and balance between incentive pull and compliance enforcement.

lowlights.title: Promecap Breach of Covenant
lowlights.body: As of February, Accounting Net Income closed at MXN -$10.5m, resulting in T3M Accounting Net Income of MXN -$11.4m. Consequently, the Promecap Profit Index stands at (8.66%), below the required threshold (>0.0%) and therefore in technical breach. This result is primarily driven by pressure in portfolio performance (particularly repossessions, collection buckets, and write-offs) which is flowing through a higher Loan Loss Reserve provision and portfolio write-offs impacting accounting results. We are already in active discussions with Promecap to obtain a waiver for both covenants (proactively covering the Default Rate, as well).

lowlights.title: Lead Generation & Conversion
lowlights.body: 7.1k new client applications (-36.0% MoM), out of which 5.4k were new client applications and 1.7k were re-engagements of applications, underperforming in our daily average (252/day vs. 300 objective, and vs. 350/day in Jan) mainly driven by Uber communications not being sent this month.

lowlights.title: Origination
lowlights.body: Signed 201 new vehicle leases (+14.9% MoM; -34.3% vs. Budget) and 45 used vehicle leases (+60.7% MoM; -10.0% vs. Target), out of which 127 were EVs (63.2% of total new leases), leading to a net portfolio of MXN $1,002.3m (+3.2% MoM; -4.7% vs. Budget). Originated 157 new leases in Uber (78.1% of total) and 44 new leases in DiDi (21.9% of total). During Feb, 142 Uber EVs completed their first trip (including originations from previous months), 70.2% of Q1 target and 10.6% of the '26 target.

lowlights.title: Portfolio
lowlights.body: Past due portfolio (+90 days) represented 3.4% (+146 bps MoM) of the total portfolio. Our LLR has an outstanding balance of MXN $41.9m, which represents 4.1% of the total portfolio and a 1.18 coverage ratio increase mainly due to interest losses from recovered vehicles and losses on repossessed vehicles.

lowlights.title: Repossessions
lowlights.body: We completed 139 effective repossessions during the period (+37 units MoM), reflecting recoveries across multiple cohorts and operational stages. Closed the month with 38 vehicles pending repossession, including carryover from prior periods. To improve execution and move toward the 7-day SLA proposed last month, we focused on: (i) enhanced coordination between Fleet and Collections teams; (ii) earlier initiation of repossession requests by Collections; (iii) prioritization of vehicles with active GPS reporting and faster recovery potential.

lowlights.title: Used Vehicle Inventory
lowlights.body: Increased to 403 units (11.1% of fleet, +91 units MoM), comprised of 196 EVs and 207 ICE-HEV. This was mainly driven by: (i) 139 effective repossessions; (ii) 45 used vehicle originations; and (iii) the reclassification of 3 vehicles as fixed assets.

lowlights.title: Collections
lowlights.body: Closed at 95.6% (91.9% without additional payments, +23 bps MoM), with Uber collections at 92.1% (-782 bps MoM) and DiDi collections at 78.2% (-1,327 bps MoM). DiDi’s collection underperformance was mainly driven by: (i) DiDi's retention tool did not collect as expected starting on Jan 12th; and (ii) Apr–Jul'25 suboptimal vintages still normalizing.

lowlights.title: Security Update
lowlights.body: (i) One vehicle (Chevrolet Aveo) was burned in Guadalajara following a prior theft, with the client unharmed and insurance coverage currently under review given the sequence of events; (ii) Uber temporarily suspended operations in Jalisco and Guanajuato (~2 days), while DiDi maintained partial service; and (iii) implemented goodwill support for affected clients in Guadalajara and Puerto Vallarta (MXN $1,500/client; ~MXN $450k total cost).

highlights.title: Lead Generation & Conversion
highlights.body: 12.6k new client applications (+77.5% MoM), out of which 10.4k were new client applications and 2.2k were re-engagements of applications, overperforming in our daily average (406/day vs. 300 objective, and vs. 252/day in Feb).

highlights.title: Referral Program
highlights.body: Since launch in August, the referral program has yielded 977 referrals, out of which 225 have resulted in originations, still representing the most efficient conversion channel with a 23.0% conversion rate (vs. our avg. conversion rate of 2.2%).

highlights.title: DAE-LTO Program
highlights.body: Transitioned 57 DAE drivers (14.0% above target), mainly driven by improved communication of the benefits of our LTO EV product.

highlights.title: Active Fleet
highlights.body: Reached 3,300 vehicles (+2.8% MoM; -1.0% vs. Budget) out of which 1,668 are active EVs (50.5% of active fleet, +5.8% MoM), with 1,332 corresponding to Uber (79.9%; 396 light and 936 Premium) and 336 to DiDi (20.1%, 185 light and 151 Premium).

highlights.title: Branding
highlights.body: Branded 105 vehicles during the month (92 Uber, 13 DiDi), bringing the total branded fleet for Uber to 406 (53.4% of the 760 target) and 65 for DiDi vehicles.

highlights.title: Customer Experience (CX)
highlights.body: Transitioned to a new operating model, maintaining stable early delinquency at 93.8%. Key initiatives include: (i) an early contact strategy (0–7 days) to improve recovery rates, (ii) deployment of on-site CX partners at hubs and Guadiana for in-person support; (iii) a tiered support model where ATC handles basic inquiries (Level 1), CX partners manage specialized follow-ups (Level 2), Collections addresses only high-complexity cases (Level 3), and (iv) ATC takeover of basic support to scale operations and allow the core team to focus on higher-complexity cases.

highlights.title: Tech Tools (Daniel AI Agent)
highlights.body: Continued scaling AI-driven lead automation through the profiling agent (“Daniel”). Since December 13th, 2025, 14,806 leads were assigned to the AI agent, of which 7,680 initiated the profiling process. Of the leads engaged by AI, 40% completed the lead profiling process, demonstrating sustained adoption of automated qualification workflows. The AI agent now processes 47% of after-hours leads, with 40% completing profiling outside business hours – extending coverage without additional headcount.

highlights.title: API Update (Uber Integration)
highlights.body: We've integrated with Uber's API, enabling real-time access to drivers' data: (i) earnings, (ii) trip-level data, and (iii) account-level information. To date, ~300 prospective clients have connected during the sales process, and 585 existing clients are integrated in the post-sales phase. Next steps include integrating with DiDi's origination tool (limited to 12 weeks of earnings) and advancing DiDi's API for post-sales to achieve visibility comparable to Uber.

highlights.title: New Product & Legacy Retention Strategy
highlights.body: We are working to complement the new product pricing (pending Board approval) with a legacy portfolio retention strategy aimed at containing potential repossessions and improving client stability. Final details will be defined during April, after which we plan to roll out the full strategy.

highlights.title: Rappi Prospect
highlights.body: Initial discussions with Rappi are underway to explore LTO scheme targeting ~25,000 “Diamond” couriers driven by rainy season pressures on 2-wheelers, increased competition with Uber Eats and DiDi Food, and the World Cup demand tailwind. Rappi has indicated willingness to support the program through levers such as: (i) lead generation; (ii) CAPEX grants; (iii) revenue guarantee; (iv) priority dispatch to ensure trip volume covers the guarantee with healthy unit economics; (v) API integration for data sharing; and (vi) direct collections from their platform. For VEMO, this represents an opportunity to diversify demand beyond ride-hailing, mitigate BYD exposure, and enhance portfolio stability; key open items include the guarantee structure, acceptance rates, anti-gaming mechanisms, and overall incentive design.

lowlights.title: Covalto Breach of Covenant
lowlights.body: As of March, Accounting Net Income closed at MXN -$9.3m, resulting in T12M Accounting Net Income of MXN -$14.2m. Consequently, the Covalto Profit Index stands below the required threshold (>0m) and therefore in technical breach. This result is primarily driven by pressure in portfolio performance (particularly repossessions, collection buckets, and write-offs) which is flowing through a higher Loan Loss Reserve provision and portfolio write-offs impacting accounting results. We are already in active discussions with Covalto to obtain a waiver for the covenant.

lowlights.title: Promecap Breach of Covenant
lowlights.body: As part of the waiver workstream, we identified an additional covenant at risk, defined as Active Fleet (Leased vehicles + Substitute vehicles being used by a client) / Total Fleet (Leased vehicles + Repossessed vehicles + Substitute vehicles), with a contractual threshold of ≥ 85.00%. Following March close, this ratio is 83.81% (3,423 / 3,962), representing a technical breach as of March. We are already in active discussions to obtain a waiver for the covenant and aligning with Promecap on the agreed calculation methodology.

lowlights.title: Origination
lowlights.body: Signed 177 new vehicle leases (-11.9% MoM; -47.3% vs. Budget) and 52 used vehicle leases (+15.6% MoM; -25.7% vs. Target), out of which 118 were EVs (66.7% of total new leases), leading to a net portfolio of MXN $1,050m (+1.6% MoM; -8.5% vs. Budget). Originated 124 new leases in Uber (70.1% of total) and 53 new leases in DiDi (29.1% of total). During Mar, 121 Uber EVs completed their first trip (including originations from previous months), 100.7% of Q1 target and 15.3% of the '26 target, resulting in MXN $10.0m Uber rebate, pursuant to the DAE/LTO agreement.

lowlights.title: Portfolio
lowlights.body: Past due portfolio (+90 days) represented 3.8% (+40 bps MoM) of the total portfolio. Our LLR has an outstanding balance of MXN $45.0m, which represents 4.2% of the total portfolio and a 1.13x coverage of our past due portfolio.

lowlights.title: Repossessions
lowlights.body: We completed 140 effective repossessions during the period (+1 unit MoM), reflecting recoveries across multiple cohorts and operational stages. Closed the month with 33 vehicles pending repossession, including carryover from prior periods.

lowlights.title: Used Vehicle Inventory
lowlights.body: Increased to 489 units (12.9% of fleet, +86 units MoM), comprised of 221 EVs and 268 ICE-HEV. This was mainly driven by: (i) 140 effective repossessions; (ii) 52 used vehicle originations; and (iii) the reclassification of 2 vehicles as fixed assets.

lowlights.title: Collections
lowlights.body: Closed at 95.1% (92.1% without additional payments, -55 bps MoM), with Uber collections at 92.4% (-43 bps MoM) and DiDi collections at 80.0% (+176 bps MoM). DiDi's collection underperformance was mainly driven by DiDi's retention tool failing to collect as expected starting on January 12th.

highlights.title: Lead Generation & Conversion
highlights.body: 13.4k new client applications (+6.3% MoM), out of which 12.0k were new client applications and 1.4k were re-engagements of applications, overperforming in our daily average (446/day vs. 433 objective, and vs. 408/day in March).

highlights.title: Referral Program
highlights.body: Since launch in August, the referral program has yielded 1,079 referrals, out of which 244 have resulted in originations, still representing the most efficient conversion channel with a 22.6% conversion rate (vs. our avg. conversion rate of 4.2%).

highlights.title: Active Fleet
highlights.body: Reached 3,384 vehicles (+2.5% MoM; -6.4% vs. Budget) out of which 1,772 are active EVs (52.4% of active fleet, +6.2% MoM), with 1,397 corresponding to Uber (77.8%; 445 light and 952 Premium) and 375 to DiDi (22.2%, 203 light and 172 Premium).

highlights.title: Branding
highlights.body: Branded 110 vehicles during the month (97 Uber, 13 DiDi), bringing the total branded fleet for Uber to 503 (66.2% of the 760 target) and 78 for DiDi vehicles.

highlights.title: Tech Tools (Daniel AI Agent Launch)
highlights.body: April marked a major milestone in AI-driven lead automation with the transition from Wattsy (third-party AI Agent) to Daniel (in-house AI Agent), an enhanced profiling agent launched on April 16th. While Wattsy operated with 20% lead allocation, Daniel's performance has enabled the system to scale ~80% of leads, supported by strong reliability and improved operational safeguards. Since activation, Daniel has managed 8,935 leads, with 4,298 reaching a finalized process status and 2,103 completing profiling; among engaged leads, profiling completion reached 48.9%.

highlights.title: New Product & Restructuring
highlights.body: We are targeting a May 18th launch for three coordinated initiatives: (i) an On-Time Payment Discount (“OTPD”) of MXN$ 400/week across EV Premium and EV Light products tied to zero past-due balance and active Uber/DiDi API connectivity, (ii) a retention strategy focused on restructuring 15–120 DPD clients into the OTPD framework, and (iii) a redesigned product offering with a more competitive entry point and stricter origination criteria. The Weekly Trip Threshold Discount (“WTTD”) has been deferred to Phase 2 to accelerate rollout and reduce execution risk while technology development continues toward the originally proposed WTTD + OTPD structure.

highlights.title: Procurement
highlights.body: The 2026 deployment plan holds at up to 4,900 units, unchanged from prior Board approval. Fleet mix shifts toward +254 BYD Dolphin Mini units (754 total), offset by -228 GAC Aion UT / Geely EX2 and -26 ICE. Weighted average IRR moves from 25.0% to 24.2% (-80 bps). The Dolphin Mini increase has two drivers: (i) 140 units purchased before the revised BYD negotiations and Board-adjusted approval closed, and (ii) 114 units to sustain LTO operations outside CDMX (Monterrey and Tijuana), bridging the ~45-day lead time on direct GAC procurement, delivery, plating, and registration, now managed internally under broader cost negotiations.

highlights.title: Rappi Prospect
highlights.body: Rappi continues to indicate willingness to support the program, but the incentive toolkit has narrowed. CAPEX grants and standalone weekly bonuses are no longer feasible due to technology and operational constraints; all incentives must now flow through Rappi's revenue guarantee structure. Active levers under evaluation: priority dispatch, API integration for operational data sharing, and direct collections through Rappi's platform. Strategically, the partnership diversifies VEMO away from Uber/DiDi concentration, adds a second demand engine, and stabilizes portfolio earnings through guaranteed structures. It also opens a path to redeploy used EV Light inventory into a dedicated delivery operating-lease vehicle for Rappi operations.

lowlights.title: Covalto Breach of Covenant
lowlights.body: We made progress aligning on a path to securing a waiver by the end of next week. The expected structure would be a waiver covering the period already breached, plus an additional three-month coverage period. Covalto also restated their interest in providing additional debt capacity, initially through an upsizing of the current facility (+MXN 200–225 million). Beyond this, they also signaled openness to taking on additional exposure, potentially by refinancing existing facilities from other lenders. Their current thinking is that, once the waiver is in place, they would want to monitor performance through July and at that point push for the line upsizing, together with an amendment to the facility agreement that addresses the covenant, rather than through an additional standalone waiver.

lowlights.title: Promecap Breach of Covenant
lowlights.body: Promecap granted a formal waiver covering Profit Margin, Default Rate (Total Portfolio), Default Rate (Pledged Portfolio), Fleet Utilization through July 1st, 2026. Following this, we held a follow-up meeting with the Promecap team to present the retention strategy initiatives, which were positively received. We subsequently shared the supporting presentation materials to obtain formal approval and continue advancing implementation.

lowlights.title: Origination
lowlights.body: Signed 218 new vehicle leases (+23.2% MoM; -40.1% vs. Budget) and 34 used vehicle leases (-34.6% MoM; -62.2% vs. Target), out of which 149 were EVs (68.3% of total new leases), leading to a net portfolio of MXN $1,051m (+2.8% MoM; -16.0% vs. Budget). Originated 165 new leases in Uber (75.7% of total) and 53 new leases in DiDi (24.3% of total). During Apr, 151 Uber EVs completed their first trip (including originations from previous months), 22.5% of Q2 target and 21.1% of the '26 target.

lowlights.title: DAE-LTO Program
lowlights.body: Transitioned 47 DAE drivers (6.0% below target).

lowlights.title: Portfolio
lowlights.body: Past due portfolio (+90 days) represented 4.8% (+100 bps MoM) of the total portfolio. Our LLR has an outstanding balance of MXN $47.4m, which represents 4.3% of the total portfolio and a 0.9x coverage of our past due portfolio.

lowlights.title: Repossessions
lowlights.body: We completed 121 effective repossessions during the period (-19 units MoM), reflecting recoveries across multiple cohorts and operational stages. Closed the month with 32 vehicles pending repossession, including carryover from prior periods.

lowlights.title: Used Vehicle Inventory
lowlights.body: Increased to 575 units (14.5% of fleet, +89 units MoM), comprised of 270 EVs and 305 ICE-HEV. This was mainly driven by: (i) 121 effective repossessions; and (ii) 34 used vehicle originations.

lowlights.title: Collections
lowlights.body: Closed at 87.8% (83.3% without additional payments, -730 bps MoM), with Uber collections at 89.2% (-320 bps MoM) and DiDi collections at 80.4% (+40 bps MoM). Low collection rate is mainly driven by implementation of early delinquency initiatives – which turned out to be more complicated than expected – and Holy Week and Easter, historically 2 of the lowest points of the year due to holidays (school break during the two weeks) and no rain. Of note, during the first week of May, we have already recovered previous collections levels of over 95% (incl. additional payments).

highlights.title: Collections
highlights.body: May is the first month that fully operated under the new collections team structure. We continue pushing retention coverage, upgrading past due balance recovery guidelines, and fine-tuning our EV product policies (retention, eligibility & after-sales services) to continue improving delinquency and repossession rates.

highlights.title: On-Time Payment Discount
highlights.body: The On-Time Payment Discount framework provides direct visibility into the operational frictions limiting payment compliance and platform connectivity adoption. Early implementation has driven a 14% increase in connected users (505 to 574 in two weeks). Key adoption frictions identified include limited visibility into past-due balances, app connectivity issues (Uber/DiDi APIs and bank account registration), and driver concerns regarding cashback reliability. In response, targeted communications, driver outreach, and accelerated bug resolution efforts are underway.

highlights.title: Restructure Program
highlights.body: Contacted 159 of 387 shortlisted clients, achieving a 56% proposal acceptance rate (89 clients), with 30 completed payments generating MXN $251k in collections to date. The process has also helped identify hardship, CX, insurance, and payment allocation issues, resolving several cases without requiring a formal restructure.

highlights.title: New Product Launch
highlights.body: All new commercial activity is now being originated under the updated pricing structure, including the On-Time Payment Discount. While no vehicles have yet been delivered under the new product due to the ~11-day origination cycle, approximately 100 contracts are currently in the signing process.

highlights.title: Used Vehicle Pricing Strategy
highlights.body: Launching in June, incorporating an enhanced repair-versus-disposition decision framework designed to improve recovered vehicle economics and support the rollout of the LTO–DAE leasing program.

highlights.title: Lead Generation & Conversion
highlights.body: 9.7k new client applications (-27.6% MoM), out of which 8.8k were new client applications and 978 were re-engagements of applications, overperforming in our daily average (313/day vs. 305 objective, however lower vs. 446/day in April).

highlights.title: Referral Program
highlights.body: Since launch in August, the referral program has yielded 1,171 referrals, out of which 273 have resulted in originations, still representing the most efficient conversion channel with a 23.3% conversion rate (vs. our avg. conversion rate of 2.4%).

highlights.title: Active Fleet
highlights.body: Reached 3,418 vehicles (+1.0% MoM; -13.0% vs. Budget) out of which 1,820 are active EVs (53.2% of active fleet, +2.7% MoM), with 1,428 corresponding to Uber (78.4%; 468 light and 960 Premium) and 392 to DiDi (21.5%, 205 light and 187 Premium).

highlights.title: Branding
highlights.body: Branded 95 vehicles during the month (90 Uber, 5 DiDi), bringing the total branded fleet for Uber to 593 (78.0% of the 760 target) and 83 for DiDi vehicles.

highlights.title: Tech Tools (Daniel AI Agent)
highlights.body: May marked a major milestone for Daniel, VEMO’s in-house AI agent, which processed 100% of inbound leads (+22.5% MoM) while maintaining a stable approval volume (310 vs. 314 in April), demonstrating consistent decisioning performance despite higher intake. Mid-conversation abandonment declined 20.5% MoM (from 4,713 to 3,749) following the deployment of recovery and reactivation workflows. Increased unresponsive leads and undelivered messages were primarily driven by higher lead volumes and changes in lead quality at the top of the funnel.

lowlights.title: Covenants (PROMECAP)
lowlights.body: PROMECAP's Default Ratio ended the month at 7.1%, above the <6.0% covenant threshold. As discussed previously, given the high likelihood of this outcome, it was proactively included in the waiver request and is therefore already captured in the current waiver.

lowlights.title: Origination
lowlights.body: Signed 161 new vehicle leases (-26.1% MoM; -59.0% vs. Budget) and 24 used vehicle leases (-29.4% MoM; -78.2% vs. Target), out of which 129 were EVs (80.1% of total new leases), leading to a net portfolio of MXN $1,062m (+1.0% MoM; -22.3% vs. Budget). Originated 125 new leases in Uber (77.6% of total) and 53 new leases in DiDi (22.4% of total). During May, 126 Uber EVs completed their first trip (including originations from previous months), 43.0% of Q2 target and 26.5% of the '26 target.

lowlights.title: DAE-LTO Program
lowlights.body: Transitioned 40 DAE drivers (20% below target).

lowlights.title: Portfolio
lowlights.body: Past due portfolio (+90 days) represented 5.8% (+100 bps MoM) of the total portfolio. Our LLR has an outstanding balance of MXN $54.0m, which represents 4.9% of the total portfolio and a 0.8x coverage of our past due portfolio.

lowlights.title: Repossessions
lowlights.body: We completed 128 effective repossessions during the period (+7 units MoM), reflecting recoveries across multiple cohorts and operational stages. Closed the month with 32 vehicles pending repossession, including carryover from prior periods.

lowlights.title: Used Vehicle Inventory
lowlights.body: Increased to 650 units (16.0% of fleet, +75 units MoM), comprised of 332 EVs and 328 ICE-HEV. This was mainly driven by: (i) 128 effective repossessions; and (ii) 24 used vehicle originations.

lowlights.title: Collections
lowlights.body: Closed at 92.7% (87.3% without additional payments, +400 bps MoM), with Uber collections at 97.9% (+870 bps MoM) and DiDi collections at 88.5% (+810 bps MoM).
